prompt:
We pull catalog data from three vendor feeds and every one of them paginates differently: vendor A sends an RFC-5988 `Link: <...>; rel="next"` header (sometimes with prev/first relations tagging along, sometimes with a relative URL), vendor B puts a `next_cursor` token in the JSON body, and vendor C does both — plus its final page comes back with zero items but still carries a cursor, so a naive loop never terminates. We have three hand-rolled page loops in the ingest service and each has grown its own bugs. I want ONE walker, package pagewalk in pagewalk.go, that handles all of them behind a bufio.Scanner-style iterator:

- `New(client *http.Client) *Walker`, `Walk(ctx context.Context, startURL string) *Iterator`, then the usual loop: `Next() bool` / `Item() map[string]any` / `Err() error`. Items come from the `items` array of each JSON page and are yielded in server order.

- Finding the next page: a Link header relation with rel="next" wins; the target may be absolute or relative (resolve relative refs against the URL you just fetched; ignore prev/first/last relations). No usable Link -> fall back to a non-empty `next_cursor` in the body: re-request the same URL with the query parameter `cursor` set to the token — replacing any cursor value already there and keeping every other parameter intact. Neither present -> the walk is done.

- An empty page (items missing, null, or []) ends the walk immediately and cleanly, even when a next link or cursor is present — that is the vendor-C trap.

- Boundary dedupe: these feeds shift under us mid-walk, so the same row shows up at the tail of one page and the head of the next. Never yield the same string `id` twice within one walk; the first occurrence wins and later repeats are dropped wholesale. Items without a string id are yielded as-is and never participate in dedupe.

- Failures: a non-2xx page (put the status code in the error), undecodable JSON, or a dead context means Next() returns false, Err() says why, and everything already yielded stands. No retry logic in here — the platform HTTP client passed to New already owns retries.

pagewalk_test.go is the acceptance contract (all local httptest servers with scripted pages, no live network). `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...` green means done. Don't touch the test file.
